eyebrow: Know what the next conversation receives
headline: Every next step includes what you will bring back.
subheadline: Define the artifact, observation, or decision the next conversation should receive—not only the action to attempt.
chatStarter: I know what to try, but not what I should observe or bring back afterward.
whyItWorks: A return condition makes follow-through concrete without turning it into surveillance.

eyebrow: A promise with a visible edge
headline: A commitment names the smallest evidence that would count.
subheadline: Describe completion through a visible result small enough to review, rather than a broad promise to make progress.
chatStarter: I keep committing to make progress, but neither of us can tell what would count.
whyItWorks: Evidence-sized commitments feel accountable without turning broad progress into a moral demand.

eyebrow: Review when reality can answer
headline: Schedule the review for when reality can answer, not Friday.
subheadline: Choose the first point when users, data, or implementation can reveal whether the decision fits.
chatStarter: Our Friday check-in happens before this decision has produced anything meaningful to review.
whyItWorks: Reality-timed review points prevent empty check-ins and premature judgments.

eyebrow: A small record that keeps the week connected
headline: One weekly note holds decision, action, observation, and adjustment.
subheadline: Keep the record short enough to maintain and complete enough to inform the next decision.
chatStarter: I want a weekly record that helps us think, not another report that takes an hour.
whyItWorks: Four short fields create continuity without a heavy reporting system.

eyebrow: Show the work before summarizing it
headline: Return with the screenshot, result, or failure—not a performance update.
subheadline: A real artifact shortens explanation and lets the review begin with what actually happened.
chatStarter: I can write a polished progress update, but the actual result would tell you more.
whyItWorks: Artifacts anchor the review in reality and reduce status-performance pressure.

eyebrow: Friction is evidence, too
headline: A missed step reveals friction; it does not grade your character.
subheadline: Inspect unclear scope, missing access, changing priorities, or hidden difficulty before deciding what should change.
chatStarter: I did not complete the step, and I want to understand what made it unrealistic without making excuses.
whyItWorks: It treats missed action as diagnostic information rather than a personal failure.

eyebrow: Keep the learning, resize the step
headline: Shrink the commitment when reality shows the week cannot carry it.
subheadline: Preserve the intended learning while reducing scope, duration, or dependencies to match available capacity.
chatStarter: The commitment still matters, but this week cannot hold its current size.
proofLine: Direct 1:1 support. Start with a free 15-minute fit check.
whyItWorks: Adaptive sizing protects continuity when real capacity differs from the plan.

eyebrow: Do not let promises accumulate silently
headline: Renegotiate one unfinished promise before adding another.
subheadline: Close, resize, defer, or replace the earlier commitment so unfinished work does not quietly accumulate.
chatStarter: We keep adding sensible next steps while the earlier ones remain open and unexplained.
whyItWorks: Renegotiation prevents invisible commitment debt without demanding rigid completion.

eyebrow: Advice should ask reality something
headline: Each recommendation leaves with one question for the work to answer.
subheadline: State what you expect to notice if the advice fits, and what would justify revision.
chatStarter: I know the recommendation, but not what real work should tell us about whether it fits.
whyItWorks: The observation question carries advice into real work as a testable claim.

eyebrow: Nothing moved is still information
headline: ‘Nothing moved’ still gives the next conversation useful evidence.
subheadline: Examine what prevented motion: priority, clarity, access, confidence, or a change in the problem itself.
chatStarter: Nothing moved since our last conversation, but the reasons probably matter more than a status label.
whyItWorks: No-motion evidence keeps accountability honest without manufacturing progress.

eyebrow: Advice meets the work
headline: Advice meets a checklist at the moment you apply it.
subheadline: Translate the recommendation into observable conditions so real work can show whether it was followed and whether it helped.
chatStarter: The recommendation makes sense, but I need a way to check its application while I work.
whyItWorks: The checklist links advice, application, and evaluation in one usable artifact.

eyebrow: Apply once before repeating everywhere
headline: Apply the advice once before repeating it across the system.
subheadline: Use one representative case to expose missing context, side effects, and instructions before wider adoption.
chatStarter: The advice seems sound, but I do not want to repeat it everywhere before seeing one real use.
whyItWorks: One application creates evidence while containing the cost of weak advice.

eyebrow: Give the experiment a way back
headline: Give each experiment a return date before daily work absorbs it.
subheadline: Choose when the result should be visible, then keep the experiment open until that date.
chatStarter: We start useful experiments, then daily work absorbs them before anyone reviews the result.
proofLine: Direct 1:1 support. Start with a free 15-minute fit check.
whyItWorks: A return date protects experiments from disappearing into daily work.

eyebrow: Inspect the step before judging the effort
headline: If the action stalls, inspect its design before judging the effort.
subheadline: Check whether the step was clear, sized, accessible, and connected to a real priority before changing the person or goal.
chatStarter: The action keeps stalling, and I want to inspect the step itself before blaming motivation.
whyItWorks: Reviewing action design preserves dignity and often reveals the fix sooner.

eyebrow: The smallest step that still teaches
headline: Choose the smallest follow-through that still teaches you something.
subheadline: Reduce the action until it can produce one observation capable of improving the next choice.
chatStarter: The full follow-through feels too large, but I still want the next action to teach us something.
whyItWorks: A teaching step keeps follow-through small while preserving useful learning.

eyebrow: One commitment, one observation
headline: A quiet weekly rhythm pairs one commitment with one observation.
subheadline: Use the pair to keep continuity without turning the week into a scorecard or reporting ritual.
chatStarter: I want a weekly rhythm that keeps the project connected without making every week a performance review.
whyItWorks: The pair creates a humane rhythm focused on observation rather than performance.

eyebrow: Keep the reason beside the action
headline: Record the decision where the work happens, not in another system.
subheadline: Attach the reason and next check to the task, code, document, or conversation that will use them.
chatStarter: Our decision log is complete, but nobody sees it when doing the actual work.
whyItWorks: In-context capture reduces reporting overhead and keeps reasoning beside execution.

eyebrow: Document while alternatives are still visible
headline: Document the reason before implementation makes the choice look inevitable.
subheadline: Preserve alternatives, constraints, and the accepted tradeoff while they are still easy to remember honestly.
chatStarter: Once we implement this, everyone will forget that other reasonable options existed.
whyItWorks: Early documentation preserves honest reasoning before outcomes distort memory.

eyebrow: Let success follow the advice
headline: The recommendation carries its success signal into the work.
subheadline: Carry the expected change beside the action so application and evaluation remain part of the same decision.
chatStarter: The recommendation reaches the work, but the original success condition does not travel with it.
proofLine: Direct 1:1 support. Start with a free 15-minute fit check.
whyItWorks: A traveling success signal prevents application from separating from evaluation.

eyebrow: Success is not the only useful ending
headline: Failure, surprise, and stopping can all complete the feedback loop.
subheadline: Define in advance what each outcome means, so an experiment remains useful without needing to succeed.
chatStarter: The test did not succeed, but I want the result to close the loop instead of feeling wasted.
whyItWorks: Multiple valid outcomes keep experiments useful and emotionally safer.

eyebrow: Follow-through is not compliance
headline: Follow-through should reduce uncertainty, not prove obedience.
subheadline: Judge the step by what it clarified, changed, or ruled out—not by whether it matched the original instruction perfectly.
chatStarter: I adapted the recommendation during the work and want to review what we learned, not whether I followed orders.
whyItWorks: It replaces compliance language with the real purpose of follow-through: learning.

eyebrow: Let the result choose what follows
headline: The current step earns the next by revealing something useful.
subheadline: Let the result choose whether to continue, adjust, deepen, or stop instead of extending the plan automatically.
chatStarter: We already planned the next step, but the current result may have changed what deserves to happen.
whyItWorks: The result, not plan momentum, earns the next commitment.

eyebrow: A commitment without a character test
headline: A commitment is a learning contract, not a character test.
subheadline: Agree what the action is meant to reveal and revisit the plan without shame when reality disagrees.
chatStarter: I want accountability that helps us learn, not a promise that makes changing the plan feel like failure.
whyItWorks: Learning-contract language creates accountability without making revision feel like failure.

eyebrow: Review at the cost-appropriate pace
headline: Set review cadence by the cost of late feedback.
subheadline: Review quickly when errors compound; wait longer when the result needs real use to become meaningful.
chatStarter: I need to know whether this decision requires daily checks or enough time to produce a real result.
proofLine: Direct 1:1 support. Start with a free 15-minute fit check.
whyItWorks: Cost-based cadence balances rapid correction with enough time for meaningful evidence.

eyebrow: Someone must notice what happened
headline: Name who watches the consequence, not only who performs the action.
subheadline: Assign someone to notice the result, compare it with expectations, and reopen the decision when necessary.
chatStarter: The action has an owner, but nobody is responsible for noticing whether it produced the intended result.
whyItWorks: Observation ownership closes the common gap between doing and learning.

eyebrow: Record the prediction before hindsight
headline: Record what you expect before the result rewrites your memory.
subheadline: Capture the predicted behavior, cost, or outcome so surprise remains visible and useful during review.
chatStarter: After every result, our original expectation becomes harder to remember honestly.
whyItWorks: Recorded expectations make hindsight bias visible during the review.

eyebrow: Revise the explanation before pushing harder
headline: When reality disagrees, update the model before adding effort.
subheadline: Ask which assumption failed, revise the explanation, and only then decide whether more effort belongs.
chatStarter: The result keeps disagreeing with our plan, and adding more effort is not resolving the mismatch.
whyItWorks: Updating the model prevents repeated effort from protecting a wrong explanation.

eyebrow: Reality before the retrospective story
headline: Begin the review with the artifact, not a polished summary.
subheadline: Inspect the build, message, log, workflow, or result before interpreting it through a retrospective story.
chatStarter: I can explain what happened, but I would rather start by showing you the actual artifact.
whyItWorks: Artifact-first review keeps feedback specific, grounded, and less performative.

eyebrow: Done and learned are different
headline: Every next move needs both a done signal and a learning signal.
subheadline: One signal confirms completion; the other shows whether the direction deserves to continue.
chatStarter: We can tell when the task is done, but not whether completing it taught us anything useful.
whyItWorks: Two signals separate task completion from whether the direction was useful.

eyebrow: Let evidence return while the question matters
headline: Scope the action so its result arrives before priorities change.
subheadline: Choose a small enough action that evidence can return while the question still matters to the project.
chatStarter: Our steps take so long that the original question changes before the evidence arrives.
proofLine: Direct 1:1 support. Start with a free 15-minute fit check.
whyItWorks: Fast-returning evidence keeps feedback relevant to the still-live decision.
